def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Beloved, precious, dear, darling; pleasing, agreeable, charming, lovable, winning; — love, darling, sweetheart, pet, favourite
subs: (-ko), v.t. To hold dear, to esteem
(-ko), v.n. To seem dear or precious (to), to be held or esteemed dear.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa 𑀧𑀺𑀬𑀸𑀭𑀬 (piyāraya-, “dear”), Sanskrit प्रियकारा (priyakārā, “agreeable”), from प्रिय (priya) + कार (kāra). By surface analysis, پیار (pyār) + ـا.

ety: Prk. पिआसा or पिवासा = S. पिपासा
def: s.f. Desire to drink, thirst; unnatural sensation of thirst (a disorder); desire, longing, craving
subs: v.n. To bear up against thirst, to endure thirst
v.n. The desire to drink to pass away, to lose the sensation of thirst.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa पिआस (piāsa), from Prakrit 𑀧𑀺𑀯𑀸𑀲𑀸 (pivāsā), from Sanskrit पिपासा (pipāsā).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Thirsty, athirst, dry; thirsting (for, -kā), desiring exceedingly, longing (for)
subs: piyāse marnā, v.n. To be dying of thirst; to be very thirsty.
wety: پیاس (pyās, “thirst”) + ـآ (-ā, adjective suffix).

def: s.f. A species of nsh.
s.f. Onion; leek.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پِیَاز (piyāz).

def: adj. Of or resembling an onion; of the flavour or colour of an onion
subs: s.m. Onion colour; a reddish colour, crimson.

def: adj. & s.m. piʼu; piyā, q.v.
s.m. The milk of a cow during the first seven days after calving, biestings; any thick fluid, cream, juice; — the food of the gods, ambrosia, nectar.
= S پييوش पीयूष pīyūsh, पेयूष peyūsh, See پيوس pīyūs, and پيوش pīyūsh.
s.m. See pīyūs.
= H پييوس पीयूस pīyūs, पेयूस peyūs, See پيوس pīyūs, and پيوش pīyūsh.
s.m. The fruit of the waved-leaf fig-tree, and of the various trees called plaksha.

s.m. The waved-leaf fig-tree, Ficus infectoria; — the holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa; — the tree Hibiscus populneoides; — a side-door, private or back door; — one of the seven dvīps or continents into which the world is divided, also called plakshu-dvīp.

adj. & s.m. Swimming, floating; — leaping, jumping, plunging; a float, raft, boat; one who goes by leaps or jumps; a tumbler; a frog; a monkey; a species of aquatic bird, Pelicanis Fusicollis; the waved-leaf fig-tree, Ficus infectoria; — a species of grass, Cyperus rotundus; — a kind of fragrant grass; — the red dye of lac.
s.m. Flowing over; submersion.
s.m. Immersion; flooding, deluging; inundation, flood, deluge.
s.m. Swimming, floating, bathing; plunging into; jumping, leaping, bounding; deluge, inundation ( = plāvan).
s.f. The spleen, &c.; v. pilaʼī
subs: (plīhā+ari), s.f. The holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa (which is deemed an enemy to the spleen).

def: s.f. = pilaʼī, q.v. adj. & s.f. Giving to drink; one who gives to drink; price paid for drinking (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dūdh-pilāʼī dāʼī, 'A wet-nurse'; — pānī-pilāʼī, 'Price paid for drinking water').

s.m. The spleen ( = pilaʼī, q.v.). adj. & s.f. Giving to drink; one who gives to drink; price paid for drinking (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dūdh-pilāʼī dāʼī, 'A wet-nurse'; — pānī-pilāʼī, 'Price paid for drinking water').
s.m. The spleen ( = plīhā)
subs: s.m. 'Destroying spleen,' or 'enemy to spleen'; the medicinal plant Andersonia rohitaka (commonly called Roherā or Rohinī).

def: s.m. The third sound given to vowels, the protracted or continuous sound of a vowel (being three times the length of a short vowel and occupying three moments in its utterance).
s.f. Bounding, leaping, capering; leap, jump, skip, hop.
s.f. The dawn ( = poh, q.v.). s.f. The first streak of the dawn, morning beam, dawn, day-break ( = po)
subs: v.n. Day to break; — poh phaṭe, adv. At day-break, at dawn.

def: s.m. A nursling of any animal; the young of a serpent; a plant; a pot-herb ( = poʼī, q.v.).
v.t. To warm by the sun or the steam of water; to bask in (the sun)
v.t. To have bread made or baked, &c.
v.t. = poṅćhnā, q.v. v.t. To wipe, dust, make clean by wiping, to clean; to wipe out, rub out, expunge
subs: (intensive), v.t. To wipe clean, wipe out, expunge.
ex: میں آپ سے کُچْھ پُوچْھنا چاہتی ہوں.
ma͠i āp se kuch pūchnā cāhtī hū̃.
I would like to ask you something.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀘𑁆𑀙𑀤𑀺 (pucchadi), from Sanskrit पृच्छति (pṛccháti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *pr̥šćáti, from Proto-Indo-European *pr̥sḱéti. Cognate with Gujarati પૂછવું (pūchvũ), Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Kalasha phúčik, Romani pućhel, Persian پرسیدن (porsīdan), German forschen.

def: s.m. An officer whose business it is to assay coin or to examine whether it is sterling or not; an examiner of coin; — a clerk who counts and examines money, a cash-keeper.
s.m. Mint, Mentha sativa
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پودنه (pūdina).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A kind of small bird Sylvia olivacea (which builds a domed nest in grass); — a dwarf, pigmy; — a ghost, demon ( = bhutnā).
adj. & s.m. Not full grown or adult, young; — a boy (one from his fifth to his sixteenth year).
s.f. The first streak of the dawn, morning beam, dawn, day-break ( = po)
subs: v.n. Day to break; — poh phaṭe, adv. At day-break, at dawn.

def: s.m. Any large domestic animal not carnivorous; — pohe, pauhe, s.m. pl. Cattle.
s.m. A flock of cotton ( = phāhā).
s.m. = paudhā, paudā, q.v. s.m. See paud and pauda.
s.m. pl. See s.v. pohā. s.m. Any large domestic animal not carnivorous; — pohe, pauhe, s.m. pl. Cattle.
v.t. To make bread, &c. ( = ponā, q.v.). v.t. To string (pearls); to thread (a needle = pironā).
s.f. The pot-herb Basella lucida, or B. rubra, or B. alba; — Anethum sowa.
v.n. To amble.
intj. Ho! holla! ho there! look out! have a care! (said by carters, drivers, &c. to warn people out of the way).
intj. Ho! holla! ho there! look out! have a care! (said by carters, drivers, &c. to warn people out of the way).
v.n. To amble.
s.m. Caterpillar, grub, worm ( = poṅkā).
s.m. = pūs, q.v. s.m. The ninth solar month of the Hindūs (Dec.-Jan., the full moon of which is near pushya, three stars in Cancer).
s.m. Nourishing, &c. = posh, q.v. s.m. Nourishing, cherishing; thriving, growth, increase; welfare, prosperity.
s.m. = poshaṇ, q.v. s.m. Nourishing cherishing, fostering, bringing up, rearing, breeding.
s.m. A pond, pool, tank, lake; a place of Hindū pilgrimage near Ajmere.
v.t. = poshnā, posnā, q.v. v.t. To nourish, &c. See poshaṇ.
s.m. A pond, pool, tank, lake; a place of Hindū pilgrimage near Ajmere.
s.m. = pukhrāj, q.v. s.m. A topaz; — the eighth nakshatra or lunar mansion.
s.m. A pole (land measure).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पोल्ल (pŏlla), from Proto-Dravidian *po-.

def: s.f. Emptiness, hollowness.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पोल्ल (pŏlla), from Proto-Dravidian *po-.

def: s.m. Gate, door ( = paur, q.v.); a courtyard; a ward or quarter of a town having its own gateway.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पोल्ल (pŏlla), from Proto-Dravidian *po-.

def: adj. Soft, flabby; hollow, having interstices, spongy, porous (cf. pulpulā); empty; emptied; light, superficial; — s.m. A hollow or superficial person; simpleton.
s.m. The finest Damascus steel (which with that of Kum is esteemed the best in the East; see fūlād).
s.m. A band or bundle of straw at the end of a staff (used with a ćarkhī to frighten and restrain a furious elephant).
s.f. A honey-comb, anything spongy; — a simpleton, blockhead.
s.m. Police.
wety: Borrowed from English police.

def: s.m. A showy dress or kind of cloth (from Jay-nagar); a wedding skirt.

ety: fr. S. प्रवय(ति), rt. वे with प्र
def: v.t. To string (pearls); to thread (a needle = pironā).
v.t. To heat (an oven, &c.); to make bread, to bake ( = pohnā).
s.m. contrac. of pahuṅćā, q.v.
s.m. Wiping, dusting; — a rag, &c. with which anything is wiped; anything thrown away after wiping; refuse, rubbish, dregs, orts.
adj. & s.m. = pūṅćhār, q.v. adj. & s.m. Having a tail, tailed; — an animal with a long tail.

ety: poṅćh˚ = Prk. पुंछ(इ) or पोंछ(इ) = S. प्रोञ्छ(ति), rt. उञ्छ् with प्र
def: v.t. To wipe, dust, make clean by wiping, to clean; to wipe out, rub out, expunge
subs: (intensive), v.t. To wipe clean, wipe out, expunge.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit प्रोञ्छति (proñchati). Cognate with Marathi पुसणे (pusṇe), Assamese মচা (mosa).

def: adj. & s.m. Empty, hollow; — a joint of bamboo, a hollow tube; — a kind of drum; — an empty-pated fellow, blockhead, simpleton.
s.m. Worm, grub ( = pokā); the ship-worm, Teredo navalis.
v.n. To be fluxed; to void liquid dung, to scour (an animal).
s.m. Mucus of the nose, snot ( = poṭā).
s.f. A species of carp, Cyprinus pausius, or C. chrysopareius.
s.f. A species of carp, Cyprinus pausius, or C. chrysopareius.
adj. & s.m. Toothless; — one who has lost his teeth.
s.f. = pulpulāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Softening food by turning it about in the mouth, turning food about in the mouth.
v.t. = pulpulānā, q.v. v.t. To soften by chewing or by turning about in the mouth, to turn food about in the mouth in order to soften it (as a person without teeth does), to mumble.
s.f. A wind instrument; a mouth-piece for certain wind instruments (composed of a rolled leaf of the Borassus); a child's whistle made of the leaf of the Borassus, or of a mango-seed.
s.m. A species of the winter cherry, Physalis angulata (sometimes called the country gooseberry: cf. papoṭan); — an eyelid ( = papoṭā).
s.f. The space or interval between two joints or articulations (of the body, or of a bamboo, sugar-cane, &c.)
subs: adv. Every joint, joint after joint; every inch.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पुर (pura).

def: s.m. A piece (of timber), block (of wood), beam, log.
ex: پورے آسمان میں راکھ پھیل گئی تھی
pūre āsamān mẽ rākh phail gayī thī
Ash had spread across the whole sky.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀽𑀭𑀬 (pūraya, “filling”), from Sanskrit पूरक (pūraka, “filling”).

def: s.m. A species of gourd, Momordica charantia.
adj. = next, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Mighty, strong, firm, stout, stiff; thick, dense; wide, broad.
s.f. Strength, firmness, stoutness, stiffness; thickness, density; width, breadth.
s.f. = por, q.v.
subs: (-kī), To cut into pieces. s.f. The space or interval between two joints or articulations (of the body, or of a bamboo, sugar-cane, &c.)
adv. Every joint, joint after joint; every inch.

def: s.f. Stiff, strong species of soil.
s.m. A joint or phalanx of the fingers, knuckle; a joint of tamarind; — also = por, q.v.
subs: v.n. To crack the joints or knuckles; — to harass.

def: s.m. = palwal, q.v. s.m. A species of cucumber, Trichosanthes diœca
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit पटोल (paṭola), ultimately from Proto-Dravidian *puḍala. Doublet of پَٹول (paṭol), a tatsama. Cognate with Marathi पडवळ (paḍvaḷ).

def: s.m. Name of a mercantile caste of Hindūs in Mālwā.
part. adj. Bred, reared; domesticated, tamed ( = hila; pālā).
s.m. Nourishing, cherishing; thriving, growth, increase; welfare, prosperity.

ety: S. पोषयति 'to wear, put on'; rt. पुष्
def: part. (of poshīdan), Covering, clothed in, dressed in, wearing (used as last member of comp., e.g. pā-posh, 'Covering the feet,' shoe, slipper; kulah-posh, 'Wearing a hat or cap'); — s.m. Covering, garment, clothing, raiment.
adj. & s.m. Nourishing, cherishing; — one who feeds or nourishes, nourisher, cherisher, supporter, breeder, keeper.
s.f. Clothes, raiment, attire, vestments, garments, dress, habit, accoutrement.
ex: ہِندُوسْتَانِی پوشَاک اَکْثَر رَنْگِیلِی ہوتِی ہَے۔
hindūstānī pośāk aksar rangīlī hotī hai.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پوشاک (pošāk).

def: adj. Fit for making garments (cloth, &c.).
s.m. Nourishing cherishing, fostering, bringing up, rearing, breeding.
adj. & s.m. = poshak, q.v. s.f. Clothes, raiment, attire, vestments, garments, dress, habit, accoutrement.

ety: perf. part. of poshīdan: see posh
def: part. adj. Concealed, hidden, secret; covered, veiled, clothed, dressed; — adv. Secretly
subs: v.n. To be concealed; to lie hid; to abscond.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پوشِیدَه (pōšīda, “covered, hidden”).

def: s.f. Concealment; secrecy; ambiguity, dubiousness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پوشِیدَگِی (pōšīdagī). By surface analysis, پوشِیدَہ (pośīda, “secret, hidden, concealed”) + گِی (gī). First attested in c. 1799 as Middle Hindi پوشِیدَگِی (pvśydgy).

def: s.f. Covering, garment, dress, clothes, &c. ( = poshāk); covering, concealing.
v.t. To nourish, &c. See poshaṇ.
adj. To be nourished or cherished, to be taken care of, to be fed, brought up, reared, &c.
subs: s.m. 'A son to be brought up,' an adopted son ( = puspūt)
s.m. A class of persons or objects to be cherished, as parents, children, guests, and the sacred fire; a family, household.

ety: pos˚ = Prk. पोस(इ) = S. पोष(ति), rt. पुष्
def: v.t. To nourish, cherish, foster, breed, rear, domesticate, tame.
s.m. Skin, hide; outer coat, rind, crust, shell; bark (of a tree); layer; poppy-head, capsule; — an intoxicating drug, an infusion of the poppy (formerly much used as a slow poison)
subs: adv. Skin upon skin, crust upon crust, layer upon layer
adj. & adv. Having the skin, &c. peeled off; — without reserve, openly, explicitly, fully, exactly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پوسْت (pōst, “skin, hide, rind, shell, cover, poppy-head”), from Old Persian 𐎱𐎠𐎺𐎿𐎫𐎠 (p-a-v-s-t-a /⁠pavastā⁠/), ultimately from Proto-Indo-Iranian *pawástam (“covering, envelope”). Cognate to Gujarati પોસ (pos). See also Sanskrit पुस्तक (pustaka).

def: s.m. One who habitually intoxicates himself with infusion of poppy-heads, one addicted to opium; a sot; a lazy indolent person; — a kind of toy (that, in whatever position it is placed, will, after wabbling about, recover its original position of stable equilibrium), a humpty dumpty (made to represent a drowsy opium eater).
adj. & s.m. Leathern, leather; — a fur garment, a shaggy leathern coat; — fault, blemish; slander, detraction
subs: s.m. A furrier, a maker of leathern garments.

def: s.m. Glass beads, beads
subs: (-kā), To make up the full complement, to make up a deficiency; to play one's part.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: s.m. Quality, disposition, nature; habit; — turn, time.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: s.m. Dung, ordure, excrement.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: s.f. A bundle, bale, package, a load ( = pushtwārā and moṭ); heap, pile, quantity; a cloth in which grain is tied up; a cloth in which a corpse is wrapped, a shroud; — a spout
subs: s.m. A species of reed, Arundo tibialis; — a kind of grass, Saccharum spontaneum.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: s.m. The treasury or the treasure-bags of government; the treasure bag of a village made up for the district treasury; — assessment on cultivated fields; rent, revenue
subs: s.m. A treasurer; — potā-dārī, s.f. The office of treasurer, treasurership.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: s.m. A son's son, grandson.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پوتا (pvta), from Prakrit *𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀅 (*pŏttaa), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑀓 (potaka /⁠pottaka⁠/), from Sanskrit पौत्र (páutra) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.m. A stick or brush used in plastering or applying a wash of mud to walls and roofs, a white-washing brush.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پوتا (pvta), from Prakrit *𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀅 (*pŏttaa), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑀓 (potaka /⁠pottaka⁠/), from Sanskrit पौत्र (páutra) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.m. = پوته pota, q.v. s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پوتا (pvta), from Prakrit *𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀅 (*pŏttaa), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑀓 (potaka /⁠pottaka⁠/), from Sanskrit पौत्र (páutra) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.m. The young of an animal; an unfledged bird; — young children; household gods.
s.m. The eyelid; the crop or craw of a bird; stomach; capacity; — mucus of the nose, snot ( = poṅṭā).
s.f. A woman with a beard; a hermaphrodite; a female servant.

s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: s.m. The treasury or the treasure-bags of government; the treasure bag of a village made up for the district treasury; — assessment on cultivated fields; rent, revenue
subs: s.m. A treasurer; — potā-dārī, s.f. The office of treasurer, treasurership.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پوتا (pvta), from Prakrit *𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀅 (*pŏttaa), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑀓 (potaka /⁠pottaka⁠/), from Sanskrit पौत्र (páutra) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.m. The scrotum ( = fot̤a, q.v.).

s.m. See s.v. pota, pot. s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).

def: s.m. The young of an animal; a young plant; a young mango; the foundation of a house.
s.f. The pot-herb Basella lucida; — a kind of bird, Turdus macrourus.
s.m. See s.v. poṭ. s.m. Glass beads, beads
subs: (-kā), To make up the full complement, to make up a deficiency; to play one's part.

def: s.m. A small glass bead ( = pot, q.v.). s.m. Glass beads, beads
subs: (-kā), To make up the full complement, to make up a deficiency; to play one's part.

def: s.m. A large book, a book, a manuscript (written on long separate leaves of paper or palmyra connected by a string through the centre).

s.m. A bird's crop, &c. = poṭā, q.v. s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).

def: s.f. Red pimples on the eyelids.
s.f. A book ( = pothā, q.v.). s.m. A large book, a book, a manuscript (written on long separate leaves of paper or palmyra connected by a string through the centre).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀣𑀺𑀬𑀸 (putthiyā), from Sanskrit *पोस्तक (*postaka), पुस्तिका (pustikā).

def: s.f. A clove of garlic.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀢𑁆𑀣𑀺𑀬𑀸 (putthiyā), from Sanskrit *पोस्तक (*postaka), पुस्तिका (pustikā).

def: s.m. A porter's load (esp. of grain).
s.f. A son's daughter, grand-daughter.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pŏttiā), from Sanskrit पौत्री (pautrī) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-).

def: s.f. The young female or any animal.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pŏttiā), from Sanskrit पौत्री (pautrī) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-).

ety: S. पुस्त+इका; or dim. of potā
def: s.f. Red cotton, or cotton wool saturated with a red dye (used for the purposes of red ink, staining, painting, &c.; syn. altā).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pŏttiā), from Sanskrit पौत्री (pautrī) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-).

def: s.f. A bird's crop, &c. = poṭā, q.v. s.m. The young of any animal; a young elephant ten years old; a young plant or tree; the young shoot of a plant; a fetus which has no enveloping membrane; cloth, a garment; the site or foundation of a house; a vessel, ship, boat
subs: potaj, s.m. An animal which is produced or developed from a fetus which has no enveloping membrane (as an elephant and certain other quadrupeds, which are supposed, in the physiology of the Hindūs, to carry their young without the aid of an ovarium or uterus).

def: s.f. A young female of any animal; — the pot-herb Basella lucida ( = pūtikā, and poʼī).
s.m. A cloth worn round the loins at the time of bathing.

s.m. A kind of toy or plaything ( = postī, q.v.). s.m. One who habitually intoxicates himself with infusion of poppy-heads, one addicted to opium; a sot; a lazy indolent person; — a kind of toy (that, in whatever position it is placed, will, after wabbling about, recover its original position of stable equilibrium), a humpty dumpty (made to represent a drowsy opium eater).
s.m. The next in turn.
s.m. One who carries a bundle or load, a porter ( = moṭiyā).
s.m. A large bundle.
s.f. A small bundle, a packet, parcel (of medicine, &c.).
s.f. A small bundle, a packet, parcel (of medicine, &c.).
v.t. To smear or wash with mud, to besmear, to plaster, to whitewash; — s.m. A stick or brush for plastering, &c. = potā, q.v.
s.m. The snout of a hog; a ploughshare.
s.m. = potṛā, q.v. s.m. The snout of a hog; a ploughshare.

ety: S. पोत 'cloth' + dim. aff. ṛā = S. रः
def: s.m. Baby-cloths, clouts
s.f. The after-birth.
s.m. lit. 'Having a snout'; a hog, boar.
v.t. = ponā, q.v. v.t. To string (pearls); to thread (a needle = pironā).
s.m. = poʼī, poʼe, q.v. s.f. The pot-herb Basella lucida, or B. rubra, or B. alba; — Anethum sowa.
s.f. Excuse, palliation, apology, pretence, pretext.
prep. (used at the beginning of comp.), Forth, for, before, forward, pre-eminent, exceeding; off, abroad, away.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺 (pari), 𑀉𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀭𑀺 (uppari), from Sanskrit उपरि (upari, “above”).

def: s.m. & adj. Joy, pleasure, delight, elation, rapture; — wanton, dissolute; mad; intoxicated (literally or metaphorically), impassioned; violent; careless (cf. pramatta and pramādī).

s.m. Flood, &c. = pravāh, q.v. s.m. Stream, current, flow, flux, course; running water; continuous flow or passage; anything moving forward like a stream; current of human affairs; course of action; usage, custom, activity; life, occupation; a drove of cattle; a pond; a beautiful or swift horse.
s.m. A young shoot, sprout, new leaf or branch; coral; the neck of the Indian lute.
adj. (f. -ā), Mighty, very powerful, of superior strength, prevailing, predominant, prevalent, violent.
v.n. To exert might, &c.
v.n. To exert might, &c.
s.m. Power, vigour, predominance, ascendency.

s.m. A connection, bond, tie; uninterrupted connection, continuous series; uninterruptedness, continuance, continuous application or action; a continued discourse or narration, connected discussion, connected story or narrative; composition, style; any literary composition, a poetical production; a kind of song; — prabandh-kalpanā, s.f. A feigned story, a work of imagination (whether founded on fact or not).
part. adj. Connected; arranged; agreed on.
s.m. Raining, causing to rain; the first rain; name of a section of a certain treatise; name of a particular mountain.
s.m. Living abroad or away from home, sojourning in a foreign country; foreign residence (temporarily); travelling; journey.
adj. & s.m. Sojourning abroad; — sojourner abroad, one who lives away from home; traveller.

s.m. = praveś, q.v. s.m. Entering (into); entrance, ingress, penetrating, penetration; admission, access (syn. daḵẖl); a religious ceremony performed previous to entering a new house, &c.; intentness (on an object), engaging closely (in a pursuit or purpose)

s.m. = praveś, q.v. s.m. Entering (into); entrance, ingress, penetrating, penetration; admission, access (syn. daḵẖl); a religious ceremony performed previous to entering a new house, &c.; intentness (on an object), engaging closely (in a pursuit or purpose)
adj. = praveśī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Going (into), entering, penetrating; — entry; an interlude.
s.f. Light, splendour, radiance, effulgence, lustre; shadow
subs: adj. & s.m. Causing light or splendour, illuminating, radiant, luminous; — the sun; the moon; fire
prabhā-vant, adj. Having light, luminous, radiant, splendid, resplendent
s.f. Name of a Sanskrit metre.

def: s.m. (in Arith.), The fraction of a fraction, a sub-fraction
subs: s.f. Reducing the fraction of a fraction to a simple fraction; reduction of sub-fractions to a common denominator.

def: s.m. Shining, light, radiance.
s.m. See s.v. prabhā. s.f. Light, splendour, radiance, effulgence, lustre; shadow
subs: adj. & s.m. Causing light or splendour, illuminating, radiant, luminous; — the sun; the moon; fire
prabhā-vant, adj. Having light, luminous, radiant, splendid, resplendent
s.f. Name of a Sanskrit metre.

def: s.m. Breaking down, destroying; air, wind, storm, tempest.
s.m. Name of a celebrated place of pilgrimage on the west coast of the Dakkhan near Dwārakā.
s.m. Morning, dawn, day-break.
adj. & s.f. Sung in a tune or rāginī peculiar to morning; — matins sung in that tune.
s.m. Superiority, pre-eminence.
s.m. Might, power, puissance, force, strength, efficacy, influence, virtue; majesty, dignity, glory, grandeur superhuma strength or power; effect; high spirit, magnanimity.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit प्रभाव (prabhāva).

def: s.m. Production, birth; origin, source, cause of existence; generative cause; the operative cause or immediate origin of being (as the father or mother); birth-place, family, lineage; strength, superiority; authority; — adj. Springing or originating (from), rising (from), derived (from), belonging (to); born, produced.
s.m. Superiority in power, authority, ascendency.
s.f. Mistress, proprietress, &c.
s.m. Splitting, cutting through; division, separation, distinction, difference, disparity; kind, sort; a nickname.
adj. Tearing asunder, cleaving, separating, severing; piercing; distinguishing, discriminating.
s.f. & adv. Beginning, commencement; — beginning with, et cetera (used at the end of comp., e.g. vr̤ihaspati-prabhr̤iti (the planets) 'beginning with Jupiter,' or 'Jupiter, et cetera').
s.m. = پربهو prabhu, q.v.
s.m. Superior, ruler, governor, master, lord, owner, proprietor, commander, principal
subs: adj. Attached or devoted to a lord, faithful to a master, loyal, dutiful
s.f. Attachment to a lord or master, loyalty, faithfulness, devotedness, dutifulness.

r: prabhu-panā, prabhut, prabhutā, prabhutāʼī, prabhutva, prabhutā karnā
def: v.n. To acquire ascendency, to prevail, to exercise sovereignty; to lord it, &c.
subs: adj. Possessing great power or rank, &c.

r: prabhut, prabhu-panā, prabhutā, prabhutāʼī, prabhutva, prabhutā karnā
def: v.n. To acquire ascendency, to prevail, to exercise sovereignty; to lord it, &c.
subs: adj. Possessing great power or rank, &c.

r: prabhutā, prabhu-panā, prabhut, prabhutāʼī, prabhutva, prabhutā karnā
def: v.n. To acquire ascendency, to prevail, to exercise sovereignty; to lord it, &c.
subs: adj. Possessing great power or rank, &c.

r: prabhutāʼī, prabhu-panā, prabhut, prabhutā, prabhutva, prabhutā karnā
def: v.n. To acquire ascendency, to prevail, to exercise sovereignty; to lord it, &c.
subs: adj. Possessing great power or rank, &c.

def: s.f. = S پربهوتتو प्रभूतत्व prabhūtatva, s.m. Abundance, plenty; multitude, great number.
s.m. = S پربهوتتا प्रभूतता prabhūtatā, s.f. Abundance, plenty; multitude, great number.

r: prabhutva, prabhu-panā, prabhut, prabhutā, prabhutāʼī, prabhutā karnā
def: v.n. To acquire ascendency, to prevail, to exercise sovereignty; to lord it, &c.
subs: adj. Possessing great power or rank, &c.

def: adj. = pravīṇ, q.v. adj. & s.m. Intelligent, skilful, knowing, clever, proticient, excellent, accomplished, conversant (with); — an able person, an adept, &c.
s.f. = pravīṇatā, q.v. s.f. = S پروينتو प्रवीणत्व pra-vīṇatva, s.m. Skill, cleverness, proficiency, sagaciousness, acuteness, shrewdness, ability.

s.m. Waking, awaking (either from sleep or from ignorance); vigilance, watchfulness, wakefulness, activity; intellect, understanding, knowledge, wisdom, intelligence; explaining; information, instruction, demonstration, producing conviction, persuading, persuasion; consoling, consolation
adj. & s.m. Awakening, arousing; making aware of, informing, convincing; — one who awakens, &c.
s.m. Awakening, arousing, exciting, reviving, recalling to life, resuscitating; instructing, &c. ( = prabodh, q.v.).
part. Awakened, awake, roused, excited; taught, instructed, informed, admonished; convinced, persuaded.
s.f. A species of the Atijagatī metre.
adj. Awake, wakened; expanded; wise, learned.
adj. = prāṅć, prāk, qq.v. adj. & adv. Turning eastward, eastern, easterly; preceding in time or place, before, in front, previous, former, prior (in comp., before certain letters, prāk becomes prāg)
subs: s.m. Previous non-existence of anything, the non-existence of anything which may yet be, antecedent privation; non-existence (of an effect) previous (to production); (in Law) the non-possession of property that may be possessed
s.m. Prior or previous existence; superiority, excellence
s.f. The eleventh Nakshatra or lunar mansion
s.m. Name of the country Kāmrūp in Assam (the scene of Brahmā's penance)
s.m. Former age or time, previous time
adj. Belonging to former or ancient times, pertaining to a previous time, anterior, ancient, previous, former
adj. & s.m. Going before, preceding; — a precursor.

def: s.m. Moving to and fro; circulating, being current or customary; activity.
part. adj. Current, customary, in vogue, circulating; prevailing, recognized, received (as authority or law).

adj. & s.m. Excessively violent, vehement, impetuous, passionate, furious, fierce, enraged, wrathful, terrible, terrific; very hot or burning; intolerable, insupportable; bold, confident, presuming; — a violent person, &c.; — a species of oleander with white flowers; name of a Dānava
subs: s.m. 'Oleander-formed,' a species of tree, Tapia cratæva.

def: s.f. = S پرچنڐتو प्रचण्डत्व praćanḍatva, s.m. Violence, vehemence; wrathfulness, fierceness; boldness.
s.m. = S پرچنڐتا प्रचण्डता praćanḍatā, s.f. Violence, vehemence; wrathfulness, fierceness; boldness.

s.m. Coming or going forth; appearing; being in actual use, currency; diffusion, spread, promulgation; appearance, manifestation, publication, disclosure, publicity, notoriety; conduct, behaviour; prevalence, currency, custom, usage; pasture-ground, pasturage; attendance on cattle while grazing
subs: (-kā), To publish, make current, diffuse, spread; to divulge, vent, let out, &c.

def: adj. Contrary to or deviating from ordinary institutions and observances, contrary to rectitude.
adj. & s.m. Making public, divulging, proclaiming, &c.; — attending, grazing cattle; — promulgator, proclaimer, herald ( = praćār-karttā).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit प्रचारक (pracāraka)

def: part. Made known, made public, made manifest, divulged, proclaimed; diffused, spread; allowed to wander or roam about; grazed; betrayed, deceived, cheated; penetrating.
part. Pursued, practised (as a profession or art).
s.m. Pupil, scholar.
v.t. = praćār karnā, q.v.s.v. praćār. adj. Contrary to or deviating from ordinary institutions and observances, contrary to rectitude.
s.m. Pupil, scholar.
s.m. (in Alg.), The common increase or difference of the terms in a progression.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پرچه (parča).

def: s.m. A wrapper, cover, covering, a blanket.
s.f. Asking, inquiring; addressing, inviting; question, inquiry.
part. adj. & s.m. Covered, enveloped; concealed, secret, hidden; clothed, clad; private, secret, unavowed, disguised; — a person in disguise; a private door, private entrance, loophole, lattice.
s.m. Yellow sandal-wood.
adj. fem. of prāṅć, prāk, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Turned towards the front, or towards the east; belonging to the front or to the east; eastern, easterly; former, prior, preceding, ancient, old; — an ancient; — s.f. Hedge, fence, wall
subs: s.m. The plant Flacourtia cataphracta, and its fruit
s.m. The sacrificial cord (worn over the right shoulder and passed under the left arm)
s.m. 'The eastern Jaka tree,' the plant Ӕgle marmelos
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit प्राचीन (prācī́na) (with the sense "old" from Classical Sanskrit).

def: s.f. A species of plant, Clypea hermandifolia; or Cissampelos hexandra.
s.f. = S پراچينتو प्राचीनत्व prāćīnatva, s.m. Oldness, antiquity.
s.m. = S پراچينتا प्राचीनता prāćīnatā, s.f. Oldness, antiquity.
s.m. Enclosure, hedge, fence, wall, rampart.
s.m. A species of Sanskr̤it metre.
s.m. Instigating, inciting; directing, enjoining, prescribing, ordering; an order; a rule, law; saying; sending.
s.f. Prickly night-shade, Solanum jacquini.
part. Impelled; incited; prescribed, commanded, directed; sent.
One who sells flour, meal, pulse, &c.; a grocer.
adj. Much, many, abundant, enough ( = bahut); abounding in, filled with, replete with; frequent.
s.f. = S پرچرتو प्रचुरत्व praćuratva, s.m. An abounding; abundance, plenty.
s.m. = S پرچرتا प्रचुरता praćuratā, s.f. An abounding; abundance, plenty.

adj. & s.m. Situated in front; belonging to the East, living in the East, eastern, &c. ( = prāćīn, q.v.); — the inhabitants of the east; the eastern country, the country south or east of the river Sarasvatī (which flows from the north-east towards the south-west).
part. Fallen (from), deviated (from), strayed.
adj. & s.m. Giving; — giver.
s.f. A gift; also = pra-da, q.v. adj. & s.m. Giving; — giver.

r: pra-daććhin, pradaćhin, pardaćhin, pra-daććhinā, par-daćhinā, pra-dakshiṇ, pra-dakshiṇā
def: s.m. = H پردچهنا प्रदच्छिणा pra-daććhinā, vulg.par-daćhinā, s.f. See pra-dakshiṇ and pra-dakshiṇā.

r: pra-daććhinā, par-daćhinā, pra-daććhin, pradaćhin, pardaćhin, pra-dakshiṇ, pra-dakshiṇā
def: s.f. = H پردچهن प्रदच्छिण pra-daććhin, pradaćhin,pardaćhin, s.m. See pra-dakshiṇ and pra-dakshiṇā.

r: pra-dakshiṇ, pra-dakshiṇā, pari-kramā, t̤awāf, pradakshiṇ karnā, kā
def: s.m. = S پردکشنا प्रदक्षिणा pra-dakshiṇā, s.f. Going round (an object), turning the right side (towards any object), reverential salutation by circumambulation from left to right (so that the right side is towards the person or object saluted; syn. pari-kramā; t̤awāf); one who is at the right hand of another; an object on the right; — pradakshiṇ karnā (-kā), To pass on the right (of), to turn the right side (towards).

r: pra-dakshiṇā, pra-dakshiṇ, pari-kramā, t̤awāf, pradakshiṇ karnā, kā
def: s.f. = S پردکشن प्रदक्षिण pra-dakshiṇ, s.m. Going round (an object), turning the right side (towards any object), reverential salutation by circumambulation from left to right (so that the right side is towards the person or object saluted; syn. pari-kramā; t̤awāf); one who is at the right hand of another; an object on the right; — pradakshiṇ karnā (-kā), To pass on the right (of), to turn the right side (towards).
s.m. Circumambulation by starting from the left and coming round to the right (by way of reverence).
s.m. = pradar, q.v. s.m. Splitting, rending; a fracture, cleft; an arrow; a particular disease of women, a flooding, mœnorrhagia.
s.m. Giving, offering, presenting, bestowing, granting; surrendering, giving up; bestowing in marriage; gift, donation; marriage.
s.m. Splitting, rending; a fracture, cleft; an arrow; a particular disease of women, a flooding, mœnorrhagia.
adj. & s.m. Foreshowing, foretelling, presaging; showing, exhibiting, displaying; proclaiming, announcing; propounding, expounding, teaching, instructing; — prophet; teacher, expounder.
s.m. Foreshowing prophesying; pointing out, exhibiting manifesting, &c.; seeing, viewing.
s.f. Offering; present of ceremony, a fee.
part. Foreshown, prophesied; shown forth, shown, brought to light, pointed out, declared, exhibited, manifested.
s.m. One who gives or bestows, giver, bestower, donor.
s.m. One who gives or bestows, giver, bestower, donor.
s.m. Plaster, a thick or viscid ointment, unguent; applying a plaster, unction.
s.m. The span of the thumb and fore-finger; — place, country.
s.m. Place, spot, region; country, district, province, foreign country; — a short span measured from the tip of the thumb to that of the forefinger.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit प्रदेश (pradeśa).

def: s.m. Gift, present, bribe; an offering.
s.f. The index finger, the forefinger; the corresponding toe.
adj. & s.m. = par-desī, q.v.s.v. para, par.
s.f. Name of one of the daughters of Daksha.

adj. & s.m. Chief, principal, leading, main, capital; pre-eminent, most eminent, most excellent, best; — a chief or principal thing, chief object, the most important thing; primary or original matter, the germ out of which all material appearances are evolved; the natural state of anything; matter; nature; the Supreme Spirit, the Supreme Deity, intellect, understanding; the first companion or attendant of a king, minister, counsellor, courtier, noble; leader, head, chief, president; head man of a village
subs: s.m. The chief element or ingredient of the body, semen virile
(pradhāna+ut˚), adj. Best of the eminent, eminent, illustrious.

def: s.f. = S پردهانتو प्रधानत्व pradhānatva, s.m. Pre-eminence, principalship, chiefship, presidentship, superiority, excellence.
s.m. = S پردهانتا प्रधानता pradhānatā, s.f. Pre-eminence, principalship, chiefship, presidentship, superiority, excellence.
s.m. Predominance, preponderance, ascendency, pre-eminence, superiority, supremacy.
s.m. Utter destruction, annihilation; loss.
adj. & s.m. Destroying, annihilating; — a destroyer.
s.f. Direction, quarter, region of the sky; an intermediate point of the compass, or half-quarter (as north-east, south-west, &c.).
s.m. A light, lamp, lantern; splendour, brilliance.
s.m. Kindling, inflaming, igniting, exciting, &c.; a sort of mineral poison (of a red colour and caustic in its action).
s.f. Direction, quarter, region of the sky; an intermediate point of the compass, or half-quarter (as north-east, south-west, &c.).
s.m. = pradosh, q.v. s.m. Defect, fault, offence, sin, transgression, guilt; the first part of the night, evening twilight, two mahūrtas after sunset.
s.m. Defect, fault, offence, sin, transgression, guilt; the first part of the night, evening twilight, two mahūrtas after sunset.
s.m. The becoming manifest or visible, arising, coming into existence, appearance, manifestation; greatness, ascendency.
s.m. Judge, chief magistrate.
s.m. 'The pre-eminently mighty-one,' an epithet of Kām-dev the god of love (see parduman).
s.m. The ancient name of Allahabad
subs: s.m. A Hindū priest of Prāg.

def: used in comp. for prāk, q.v.
adj. Bold, confident; resolute, energetic; prompt, ready; spirited, courageous, brave, intrepid, daring; audacious, proud, arrogant; strong, able; impudent, shameless; eminent.

s.f. = S پرگلبهتو प्रगल्भत्व pra-galbhatva, s.m. Boldness, confidence; energy, resoluteness, resolution; audacity, arrogance; power, eminence, consequence, prevalence; perverseness, wilfulness; impudence, shamelessness.

s.m. = S پرگلبهتا प्रगल्भता pra-galbhatā, s.f. Boldness, confidence; energy, resoluteness, resolution; audacity, arrogance; power, eminence, consequence, prevalence; perverseness, wilfulness; impudence, shamelessness.
s.m. Boldness, confidence; resoluteness, determination; pride, arrogance, effrontery; pomp, parade, rank; proficiency.
s.m. Proceeding; progress, advance.
adj. & s.m. Setting out, being about to depart; one who is about to depart.
adj. & s.m. Much, excessive; hard, difficult; hard, firm, steady; serious; — pain, privation, penance.
(S. pra-gāḍhatā), s.f. = H پرگاڙهتو प्रगाढ़त्व pra-gāṛhatva (S. pra-gāḍhatva), s.m. Abundance, excessiveness; hardness, difficulty; firmness, steadiness; seriousness.
(S. pra-gāḍhatva), s.m. = H پرگاڙهتا प्रगाढ़ता pra-gāṛhata (S. pra-gāḍhatā), s.f. Abundance, excessiveness; hardness, difficulty; firmness, steadiness; seriousness.
adj. = prakaṭ, q.v. adj. Evident, clear, manifest, apparent, obvious, visible; unfolded, displayed, open; public, issued out, commonly known, undisguised
subs: v.t. To make evident, make known, to bring to light, divulge, disclose ( = prakāś karnā)
v.n. To become manifest; to appear, &c.

def: s.m. A singer.
= H پرگٿنا प्रगटना pragaṭnā, See prakaṭatā; and prakaṭnā.
= H پرگٿتا प्रगटता pragaṭatā, See prakaṭatā; and prakaṭnā.
s.m. A singer.
adj. & s.m. Singing; — a singer.
s.m. = praghaṇ, q.v.
s.m. Covered terrace before the door of a house; porch, portico; a copper pot; an iron mace or crowbar; a species of bean, Phaseolus mungo.
adj. = pargaṭ; prakaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = prakaṭatā, q.v. s.f. = S پرکٿتو प्रकटत्व pra-kaṭaṭva, s.m. Visibility, apparency, manifestation, clearness, publicity.
s.f. An iron trough into which silver or gold is cast previous to working it, a goldsmith's mould, &c.
s.f. = prakaṭatā, q.v. s.f. = S پرکٿتو प्रकटत्व pra-kaṭaṭva, s.m. Visibility, apparency, manifestation, clearness, publicity.
s.m. Protection, preservation, salvation.
s.m. The highest point, summit
subs: adj. Taking the best share, taking the lead, going before; chief, principal.

def: s.m. Taking, seizing, holding, assuming; shutting up, confining; the commencement of an eclipse.
adj. & s.m. Chief, principal; — a chief.
